name: water_licensing_backlog_forces_staff_southport_car_fair
premise: A Water Infrastructure response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving water licensing backlog forces staff southport car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Priya Chen
agents.identity: I am Priya Chen, accountable for a defensible call in Water Infrastructure when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Elliot Reed
agents.identity: I am Elliot Reed, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Water Infrastructure.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Priya Chen must decide under care vs fairness. What does Priya Chen do?
